action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
Oh, the candy shop is calling, with a wrapper bright and sweet,
A chocolate bar, a lollipop, a treat that's hard to beat!
You grab a bag of gummy bears and stuff them in your mouth,
But wait! Your tummy starts to rumble, and you're feeling quite uncouth.
It's a sugar rush, a wobbly dance, a wiggle in your knee,
But then the crash comes crashing down, and you can't move, you see!

SECTION 2
SECTION 2
Now your teeth are doing tap-dance, but they're tapping on the wrong,
Because the sugar bugs are marching, and they sing a sticky song!
They build a castle in your molars, a fortress made of goo,
And if you don't brush them off, they'll turn your smile to blue!
The dentist says, "Oh no, no, no, that cavity is deep!"
While you're trying to hide your frown and make a promise to keep.

SECTION 3
SECTION 3
You try to run a race today, but your legs are made of jelly,
Your brain is foggy, slow, and dull, like a sleepy, sleepy mule-ly.
You wanted to be fast and strong, to jump right up to the sky,
But all you did was trip and fall, and watch the clouds go by.
The energy was just a lie, a trick that sweets will play,
Now you're yawning at the playground, wishing you could stay.

SECTION 4
SECTION 4
So listen close, my little friend, before you eat the treat,
A little bit is just okay, but don't make it a feat!
Eat an apple, eat a carrot, drink some water, cool and clear,
Then you'll have the energy to run and jump and cheer!
Save the candy for a special day, a party, or a prize,
And keep your tummy happy, healthy, and your smile in the skies!
action.label: response
